title: Reserves Adequacy & Import Cover

current.importCoverNote: Computed from SBP-held reserves of $18.47bn and the trailing 12-month average monthly goods-import bill of $5.29bn.
current.importCoverBasis: SBP-held reserves divided by the trailing 12-month average of official SBP monthly goods imports.

benchmark.label: Common minimum rule-of-thumb
benchmark.note: Three months of imports is a widely used minimum rule-of-thumb. It is not a substitute for the IMF's broader reserve-adequacy assessment.

imfTarget.note: The IMF's end-FY25 SBP reserves target was $13.9bn; Pakistan exceeded it at $14.5bn. Reserves have since been rebuilt further.

drivers: IMF EFF + RSF disbursements (~$1.3bn in May 2026)
Rollovers of bilateral deposits, notably from China and the Gulf
A contained current-account balance and stronger remittances
Tighter monetary policy supporting the rupee and inflows
context: The latest official SBP reserve stock covers about 3.5 months of the trailing goods-import bill on this dashboard's stated calculation. This is a transparent directional measure, not the IMF's broader reserve-adequacy metric.
methodologyNote: The current 3.5-month figure is calculated as $18.47bn of SBP-held reserves divided by $5.29bn, the trailing 12-month average of SBP monthly goods imports through 2026-05. It therefore measures goods-import cover and may differ from official or IMF measures that use broader imports or reserve-adequacy methods. Historical trajectory points are reported estimates and are not recalculated on the same basis.
